Explanation from Bill:
You may also see SO (Special Order), this is similar to VDF, execpt is RSA fulfillment method #2. In this scenerio the vendor first ships the item to LD, and then LD to the customer. Stock is still assigned to 101, but the fulfillment method changes from DropShip to LondonDrugs, and an attribute/property isSpecialOrder becomes true.
 
 Both VDF and SO trigger a message on the PDP and Shipping indicating the item is a special order, and to allow 3-6 weeks for delivery.

Funds are captured upfront and VDF Assigned to 101
Explanation from Bill: VDF = vendor direct fulfillment. in this case LD does not have stock, and when ordered the vendor warehouse ships direct to the customer. These are known as an "RSA Fulfillment method #1" and will translate to Kibo as Shopping Limitation = OnlineOnly and fulfilment method = "DropShip" (amongst others) these items will have stock assigned to location 101 to support sales and allocations.

Explanation from Bill: OWL stands for Overweight Limit, and is designed to help assgin higher shipping cost to items that are designated as over size/weight. Examples would be huge screen TVs, refrigerators, large furniture. There are 4 values for this, OWL-30, OWL-45, OWL-60, OWL-75 and they are assinged at the supply chain level and passed to Kibo by RSA. If you look in the custom API extensions there is a shipping section where these are referenced, along with the price upcharge for shipping. Important to note that items that have an OWL will not trigger free shipping if over $75
